metadata.title: All Training via SkyPilot — Execution Plan
metadata.description: Route all minivess training through SkyPilot with two compute paths: (A) Local GPU via SSH node pool (8 GB variants), and (B) Cloud spot instances via managed jobs (cloud variants). Fix the 5 identified gaps in train_generic.yaml and prefect_sky_tasks.py. Add Docker-inside-SkyPilot support for Prefect flow isolation.

verify_commands.command: uv run ruff check src/minivess/compute/ tests/unit/compute/

task.title: Document and script local GPU SSH node pool setup (RTX 2070 Super / 8 GB)
task.description:
SkyPilot can target the local workstation's GPU via SSH Node Pools. This enables a uniform `sky launch` interface for all training, whether local or cloud. Create a one-time setup script and README for registering localhost.
Key insight: SSH node pools use `sky launch` (NOT `sky jobs launch`). Managed jobs (spot recovery) are for cloud VMs only. For local GPU, use plain `sky launch --infra ssh/local-gpu ...`.
Note on kubectl dependency: SkyPilot SSH node pools currently require kubectl on the client machine even for non-Kubernetes workflows. This is a known architectural coupling in SkyPilot's SSH backend.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/compute/test_ssh_pool_config.py: - test_ssh_pool_yaml_is_valid: load configs/sky/local-workstation.yaml with yaml.safe_load(); assert required keys: 'hosts' (list) or 'infra' - test_sky_launch_dryrun_local: run `sky launch --dryrun deployment/skypilot/train_sam3_8gb.yaml` as a subprocess; assert exit code 0 (dry-run validates YAML structure) - test_setup_script_executable: assert scripts/setup_sky_ssh_pool.sh is executable (os.access with os.X_OK) - test_train_sam3_local_sky_has_fallback: read scripts/train_sam3_local_sky.sh via pathlib.Path.read_text(); use str.partition('force-direct') to verify --force-direct flag exists (fallback without SkyPilot) NOTE: Use pathlib.Path and str methods. No regex (CLAUDE.md Rule #16).
tdd_spec.green_phase:
Create configs/sky/local-workstation.yaml: # SkyPilot SSH Node Pool — Local GPU local-gpu: user: ${USER} identity_file: ~/.ssh/id_rsa hosts: - localhost # or use $(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}') — localhost support unverified in SkyPilot docs
Create scripts/setup_sky_ssh_pool.sh: - Check SSH server is running (systemctl is-active ssh) - If not running: print instructions (do NOT auto-enable; user must consent) - Check kubectl is installed (sky ssh up dependency) - Print `sky ssh up` command with explanation - Print `sky show-gpus --infra ssh/local-gpu` to verify GPU detection - Print `sky launch deployment/skypilot/train_sam3_8gb.yaml --dryrun` to test
Update deployment/skypilot/train_sam3_8gb.yaml to reference configs/sky/ pattern.
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - YAML configs validate (yaml.safe_load) - Dry-run YAML validation passes - Setup script is executable and prints actionable instructions - train_sam3_local_sky.sh has --force-direct fallback

task.title: Switch /checkpoints from MOUNT to MOUNT_CACHED in all cloud SkyPilot YAMLs
task.description:
Gap G1: train_generic.yaml and train_hpo_sweep.yaml use `mode: MOUNT` for /checkpoints. SkyPilot benchmarks show MOUNT_CACHED delivers 9.6x faster checkpoint writes by writing to local NVMe first, then async-syncing to S3 via rclone VFS cache. (Benchmark context: Llama 7B, 8×A100, 30-min intervals. Gain for smaller MONAI checkpoints (~200 MB–2 GB) will be lower but async-write still helps under spot.) Critical for cloud spot training where checkpoint frequency mitigates preemption loss.
Also add `disk_tier: best` to request NVMe storage (required for MOUNT_CACHED perf).

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/compute/test_sky_yaml_correctness.py: - test_cloud_yamls_use_mount_cached: load each cloud YAML with yaml.safe_load(); for each file_mounts entry with 'name' (= S3 bucket mounts), assert mode == 'MOUNT_CACHED' Files to check: train_generic.yaml, train_hpo_sweep.yaml - test_cloud_yamls_have_disk_tier_best: load YAMLs; assert resources.disk_tier == 'best' - test_local_yaml_has_no_s3_mount: load train_sam3_8gb.yaml; assert no 'store: s3' entry (local YAML uses local paths, not S3 mounts) NOTE: Use yaml.safe_load() for all YAML parsing. No regex.
tdd_spec.green_phase:
Update deployment/skypilot/train_generic.yaml: /checkpoints: name: minivess-checkpoints store: s3 mode: MOUNT_CACHED # was MOUNT resources: disk_tier: best # add NVMe for cache
Update deployment/skypilot/train_hpo_sweep.yaml: same change Update deployment/skypilot/finetune_vesselfm.yaml: already has MOUNT_CACHED (verify)
Add comment in each YAML explaining the benchmark: "9.6x faster vs plain MOUNT"
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - All cloud YAMLs use mode: MOUNT_CACHED for checkpoint buckets - disk_tier: best present in resources - Tests pass - yaml.safe_load validates all modified files

task.title: Fix MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI in cloud SkyPilot YAMLs — placeholder for public URI
task.description:
Gap G2: train_generic.yaml hardcodes MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: http://mlflow:5000 which is a Docker Compose internal hostname — unreachable from AWS/RunPod/Lambda instances. For cloud training, the tracking URI must be publicly routable.
Options (in order of simplicity): A) ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} passthrough — inject at launch time B) S3 artifact store — s3://minivess-mlruns/ C) Tunnel — documented in skypilot-advanced-plan.md section 6
This task implements Option A (simplest, covers all cases) and documents B and C.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/compute/test_sky_yaml_correctness.py (extend): - test_cloud_yamls_use_env_placeholder_for_mlflow: load each cloud YAML with yaml.safe_load(); assert envs.MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI uses ${...} substitution syntax (not hardcoded http://mlflow:5000) Use str.startswith('${') check on the YAML value, not regex. - test_local_yaml_uses_file_backend: load train_sam3_8gb.yaml; verify MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI contains 'file://' or starts with 'file:' (local file backend, no network required for local SSH runs) - test_sky_launch_passes_mlflow_uri: verify scripts/train_sam3_local_sky.sh contains 'MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI' passed via --env to sky launch (use pathlib.Path.read_text() + str.find() — no regex)
tdd_spec.green_phase:
Update deployment/skypilot/train_generic.yaml envs section: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI:-http://mlflow:5000} # IMPORTANT: Set MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI before launching: # export MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=http://YOUR_PUBLIC_IP:5000 # OR: export MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=s3://minivess-mlruns/ # See docs/planning/skypilot-advanced-plan.md section 6 for all options.
Update train_hpo_sweep.yaml: same change Update finetune_vesselfm.yaml: already has ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI:-...} (verify)
Create docs/ops/mlflow-remote-tracking.md with the three setup options.
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - Cloud YAMLs use ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI:-...} substitution (not hardcoded URI) - Local YAML uses file:// backend by default - Docs file created - Tests pass

task.title: Replace time.sleep polling in wait_sky_job with sky.stream_and_get()
task.description:
Gap G3: prefect_sky_tasks.py wait_sky_job() uses time.sleep(30) polling to check job status, losing all log streaming. SkyPilot's sky.stream_and_get() blocks until the job completes and streams logs to stdout — the Prefect task then logs them.
Also: SkyPilotLauncher.launch_training_job() calls sky.jobs.launch() which returns a request_id (not a job_id). sky.stream_and_get(request_id) blocks until done. The wait_sky_job() polling task becomes unnecessary after this change.
Requires SkyPilot 0.10+ (client-server SDK where most API calls are async and return request_ids). In older 0.9.x, sky.jobs.launch() was synchronous.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/compute/test_prefect_sky_tasks.py: - test_launch_sky_training_calls_stream_and_get: mock sky.jobs.launch() and sky.stream_and_get(); call launch_sky_training({...}); assert stream_and_get called with the request_id returned by launch() - test_wait_sky_job_deprecated: import wait_sky_job; assert it has a deprecation warning in its docstring or raises DeprecationWarning - test_launch_sky_training_dry_run: call launch_sky_training({...}, dry_run=True); assert sky.jobs.launch NOT called; assert returned status == "dry_run"
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - launch_sky_training calls sky.stream_and_get (verified by mock) - wait_sky_job issues DeprecationWarning - dry_run still works without calling sky - Tests pass, mypy clean

task.title: Create cloud training YAML for all model families (single-GPU cloud variant)
task.description: train_generic.yaml is parameterized via MODEL_FAMILY env var but defaults to dynunet. Create a unified deployment/skypilot/train_cloud_all.yaml that makes the model-family param explicit and supports all families (dynunet, mamba variants, sam3 cloud variants). SAM3 cloud variants need 16+ GB (sam3_hybrid full, sam3_topolora with full encoder). This YAML handles cloud spot (managed jobs); local 8GB uses train_sam3_8gb.yaml.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Extend tests/unit/compute/test_sky_yaml_correctness.py: - test_train_cloud_all_yaml_valid: load deployment/skypilot/train_cloud_all.yaml with yaml.safe_load(); assert keys: name, resources, envs, setup, run - test_train_cloud_all_has_model_family_env: verify envs.MODEL_FAMILY exists - test_train_cloud_all_failover_includes_runpod: verify resources.any_of list contains at least one entry with cloud: runpod - test_yaml_dryrun: subprocess `sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/train_cloud_all.yaml --dryrun` exits 0
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - YAML validates with yaml.safe_load() - Dry-run exits 0 - All model families can be passed via --env MODEL_FAMILY= - Tests pass

task.title: Update all training .sh scripts to dispatch via SkyPilot (with direct fallback)
task.description:
Currently scripts/train_*.sh call `uv run python scripts/run_training_flow.py` directly. The target architecture routes ALL training through SkyPilot with a direct fallback for environments without SkyPilot credentials:
Priority dispatch logic (same pattern as train_sam3_local_sky.sh): 1. If SkyPilot installed + cloud credentials (sky check passes for any cloud): sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/train_cloud_all.yaml --env MODEL_FAMILY=X 2. Else if SkyPilot SSH pool configured (sky check ssh passes): sky launch --infra ssh/local-gpu deployment/skypilot/train_sam3_8gb.yaml 3. Else fallback: uv run python scripts/run_training_flow.py --model-family X
Scripts to update: - train_all_best.sh (3 families) - train_dynunet.sh (1 family) - train_mamba_variants.sh (comma_mamba, ulike_mamba) - train_alternative_variants.sh (vesselfm, comma_mamba, ulike_mamba) - train_sam3_all_variants.sh (sam3_vanilla, sam3_topolora, sam3_hybrid)

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/compute/test_training_scripts.py: - test_all_training_scripts_have_sky_dispatch: for each train_*.sh, use pathlib.Path.read_text() + str.find() to verify it contains 'sky launch' OR 'sky jobs launch' OR 'sky check' (routing logic) - test_all_training_scripts_have_fallback: for each script, verify 'run_training_flow.py' still appears (fallback path) - test_scripts_are_executable: for each script, os.access(path, os.X_OK) NOTE: Use pathlib.Path and str methods. No regex (CLAUDE.md Rule #16).
tdd_spec.green_phase:
Extract shared _dispatch_training() shell function into a sourced helper scripts/_sky_dispatch.sh:
# Source this file in training scripts to get _dispatch_training() _sky_dispatch() { local model_family="$1" local loss_name="${2:-cbdice_cldice}" local max_epochs="${3:-100}"
if command -v sky &>/dev/null; then if sky check 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "enabled"; then # Cloud credentials available: use managed spot jobs sky jobs launch "${PROJECT_ROOT}/deployment/skypilot/train_cloud_all.yaml" \ --name "mnvss-${model_family}-$$" \ --env "MODEL_FAMILY=${model_family}" \ --env "LOSS_NAME=${loss_name}" \ --env "MAX_EPOCHS=${max_epochs}" \ --env "MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI:-}" \ --down --yes return $? elif sky check 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "ssh"; then # SSH node pool only: local GPU sky launch "${PROJECT_ROOT}/deployment/skypilot/train_cloud_all.yaml" \ --infra "ssh/${SKY_POOL:-local-gpu}" \ --name "mnvss-${model_family}-$$" \ --env "MODEL_FAMILY=${model_family}" --env "LOSS_NAME=${loss_name}" \ --env "MAX_EPOCHS=${max_epochs}" --down --yes return $? fi fi # Fallback: direct execution uv run python "${PROJECT_ROOT}/scripts/run_training_flow.py" \ --model-family "${model_family}" \ --loss-name "${loss_name}" \ --max-epochs "${max_epochs}" \ --trigger-source "$(basename "$0")" }
Update each train_*.sh to source _sky_dispatch.sh and call _dispatch_training().
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - All training scripts contain SkyPilot dispatch logic - All scripts retain direct fallback - Tests pass - Scripts remain executable

task.title: Add per-model-family VRAM routing: auto-select local vs cloud YAML
task.description:
Different model families have different VRAM requirements. The dispatch logic should automatically select the appropriate YAML: - 8GB models (dynunet, mamba, sam3_vanilla, sam3_topolora): try local SSH first - Marginal models (sam3_hybrid at ~7.5GB): try local with warning - Cloud-only models (vesselfm ~10GB, sam3 full fine-tune 16GB+): cloud only
This avoids OOM errors and provides helpful guidance when local GPU is insufficient.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/compute/test_vram_routing.py: - test_dynunet_routed_to_local_or_cloud: get_target_yaml("dynunet") returns either train_sam3_8gb.yaml (local) or train_cloud_all.yaml (cloud) — both are valid - test_vesselfm_routed_to_cloud_only: get_target_yaml("vesselfm") never returns the local 8GB YAML — it requires cloud GPU - test_sam3_hybrid_emits_warning: get_target_yaml("sam3_hybrid") for local target returns local YAML but logs a warning about 7.5 GB usage
tdd_spec.green_phase: Create src/minivess/compute/vram_routing.py: - VRAM_BUDGET_GB: dict[str, float] mapping model_family to estimated VRAM - LOCAL_GPU_VRAM_GB = 8.0 (RTX 2070 Super) - LOCAL_HEADROOM_GB = 0.5 (SkyPilot runtime + OS) - get_target_yaml(model_family, infra="auto") -> tuple[str, str]: Returns (yaml_path, infra_hint) based on VRAM budget vs local limit. infra_hint is "ssh/local-gpu", "cloud_spot", or "cloud_only" - Integrate into _sky_dispatch.sh via a uv run python call
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - get_target_yaml() returns correct YAML for each model family - VesselFM and SAM3 full fine-tune always routed to cloud - Warning logged for marginal (sam3_hybrid) on local - Tests pass, mypy clean

task.title: Add Docker-in-SkyPilot mode: run Prefect training_flow() inside Docker on cloud VM
task.description:
Target architecture: SkyPilot provisions a cloud VM, then runs the Prefect training flow inside a Docker container (minivess-train:latest) for full dependency isolation.
Benefits: - Exact same environment as local Docker Compose development - No uv sync on remote (just docker pull) - Prefect flow runs inside container, MLflow writes to /app/mlruns (mounted)
The run: block in train_cloud_all.yaml gets a Docker wrapper: docker run --gpus all \ -e MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI="${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}" \ -v /checkpoints:/app/checkpoints \ -v /data:/app/data:ro \ minivess-train:${IMAGE_TAG:-latest} \ uv run python scripts/run_training_flow.py --model-family "${MODEL_FAMILY}"
This requires the image to be accessible from cloud instances (Docker Hub or GHCR).

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/compute/test_docker_in_sky.py: - test_train_cloud_docker_yaml_valid: load deployment/skypilot/train_cloud_docker.yaml with yaml.safe_load(); assert 'docker run' in run section - test_docker_run_has_gpu_flag: use str.find('--gpus all') on run block content (pathlib.Path.read_text() + yaml.safe_load() + str.find()) - test_docker_image_tag_is_parameterized: verify IMAGE_TAG appears in envs section (use str.find() on the YAML content — not hardcoded 'latest') - test_docker_mounts_match_file_mounts: /data and /checkpoints in docker run -v args correspond to file_mounts in the YAML (cross-check)
tdd_spec.green_phase:
Create deployment/skypilot/train_cloud_docker.yaml: - Same resources/file_mounts as train_cloud_all.yaml - envs adds: IMAGE_TAG: ${IMAGE_TAG:-latest} - setup installs Docker if not present (apt-get docker.io on Ubuntu cloud VMs) - setup pulls image: docker pull ghcr.io/minivess-mlops/minivess-train:${IMAGE_TAG} - run uses docker run --gpus all with volume mounts from file_mounts paths
Document in YAML: Docker Hub image must be pushed before launching. Add build+push step to GitHub Actions CI (separate PR).
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - train_cloud_docker.yaml validates and dry-runs successfully - Docker run command includes --gpus all and correct volume mounts - IMAGE_TAG is parameterizable - Tests pass

task.title: Add Docker image push step to CI for SkyPilot cloud pulls
task.description:
For SkyPilot cloud VMs to pull minivess-train:latest, the image must be in a public registry (Docker Hub or GHCR). Add a GitHub Actions step that builds and pushes Dockerfile.train to GHCR on main branch merges.
This is a prerequisite for T-08 to work on cloud instances.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/compute/test_ci_docker_push.py: - test_docker_push_workflow_exists: assert pathlib.Path('.github/workflows/docker-push.yml').exists() - test_docker_push_targets_train_image: load workflow YAML; use yaml.safe_load(); verify Dockerfile.train is referenced in the build steps - test_docker_push_uses_ghcr: verify ghcr.io/minivess-mlops appears in the workflow (str.find() on yaml content)
tdd_spec.green_phase: Create .github/workflows/docker-push.yml: name: Build and Push Training Docker Image on: push: branches: [main] paths: - 'deployment/docker/Dockerfile.train' - 'pyproject.toml' - 'uv.lock' jobs: push: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: docker/login-action@v3 with: registry: ghcr.io username: ${{ github.actor }} password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} - uses: docker/build-push-action@v5 with: context: . file: deployment/docker/Dockerfile.train push: true tags: ghcr.io/minivess-mlops/minivess-train:latest
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - Workflow file exists and validates - Builds Dockerfile.train and pushes to GHCR - Tests pass

task.title: Create SkyServe YAML stub for ONNX/BentoML inference (P2 — closes #420)
task.description:
P2 issue #420: SkyServe deploys inference replicas as auto-scaled multi-cloud HTTP services. Create a stub YAML for the BentoML/ONNX Runtime inference service.
Key design decisions: - min_replicas: 0 for scale-to-zero (idle periods between experiments) - readiness_probe: /healthz (BentoML) or /v2/health/ready (Triton/ONNX Server) - target_qps_per_replica: 1.0 (conservative — 3D segmentation is slow) - accelerators: {L4:1, A10g:1, RTX-3090:1} — cheapest GPUs with enough VRAM
NOTE: SkyServe support for SSH node pool backends is poorly documented. Official docs say "services" run on SSH pools, but practical limitations (load balancer, public endpoints) may apply. Use cloud VMs or Kubernetes for production SkyServe deployments. The local RTX 2070 Super cannot serve as a SkyServe replica.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/compute/test_sky_serve_yaml.py: - test_serve_onnx_yaml_valid: load deployment/skypilot/serve_onnx.yaml with yaml.safe_load(); assert required keys: service, resources, run - test_serve_has_scale_to_zero: load YAML; assert service.replica_policy.min_replicas == 0 - test_serve_has_readiness_probe: assert service.readiness_probe exists and is non-empty - test_serve_has_port: assert resources.ports is defined (HTTP port for inference) - test_serve_dry_run: subprocess 'sky serve up deployment/skypilot/serve_onnx.yaml --dry-run'; assert exit 0
tdd_spec.green_phase:
Create deployment/skypilot/serve_onnx.yaml: service: readiness_probe: /healthz replica_policy: min_replicas: 0 max_replicas: 4 target_qps_per_replica: 1.0 upscale_delay_seconds: 60 downscale_delay_seconds: 600
resources: ports: 8080 accelerators: {L4:1, A10g:1, RTX-3090:1} disk_size: 50
setup: | pip install bentoml onnxruntime-gpu huggingface_hub # Pull champion ONNX model from MLflow registry python -c "from minivess.serving.bento_model_import import pull_champion; pull_champion()"
run: | bentoml serve minivess_service:latest --port 8080
Create deployment/skypilot/serve_bentoml.yaml: variant with BentoML serve
Add scripts/serve_champion_sky.sh: sky serve up deployment/skypilot/serve_onnx.yaml -n minivess-serve
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - YAML validates and dry-run exits 0 - scale-to-zero (min_replicas: 0) configured - readiness_probe set - Tests pass - P2 issue #420 closed (P0 milestone)

task.title: Integration test: end-to-end SkyPilot dispatch for all model families
task.description: Verify the complete routing logic works for all model families (dry-run mode): - Dry-run tests that SkyPilot YAMLs are valid and sky commands would succeed - Unit tests that vram_routing.py routes correctly per family - Smoke test that _sky_dispatch.sh fallback works without credentials

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/integration/compute/test_sky_dispatch_integration.py: - test_dry_run_train_generic: subprocess sky jobs launch train_generic.yaml --dryrun; exit 0 - test_dry_run_train_cloud_all: subprocess sky jobs launch train_cloud_all.yaml --dryrun; exit 0 - test_dry_run_train_sam3_8gb: subprocess sky launch train_sam3_8gb.yaml --dryrun; exit 0 - test_dry_run_finetune_vesselfm: subprocess sky jobs launch finetune_vesselfm.yaml --dryrun; exit 0 - test_dry_run_serve_onnx: subprocess sky serve up serve_onnx.yaml --dry-run; exit 0 - test_vram_routing_all_families: for each ModelFamily enum value, get_target_yaml() returns a valid file path (pathlib.Path exists check) - test_dispatch_fallback_without_sky: unset SKYPILOT env; set PATH to exclude sky; assert _dispatch_training() falls back to run_training_flow.py call
tdd_spec.green_phase: Fix any issues found by the dry-run tests. Ensure all YAML files validate against SkyPilot's schema expectations. Verify fallback paths work without credentials.
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - All sky --dryrun commands exit 0 - VRAM routing covers all ModelFamily enum values - Fallback works without SkyPilot installed - Integration tests pass in CI (CI has no SkyPilot credentials — tests dry-run only)

wave.description: Routing — all scripts dispatch via SkyPilot

statistics.gaps_deferred: G4 (done in PR #403) G5 (done in finetune_vesselfm.yaml)
